title: hierochloe odorata subsp. hirta
intro: Hierochloe odorata subsp. hirta, also known as sweet grass, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its sweet, vanilla-like fragrance. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Sweet grass is said to have various health benefits, including reducing stress and anxiety, promoting digestion, and soothing respiratory issues. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and support overall well-being.
